visa_on_arrival.name: Bahamas
visa_on_arrival.notes: Some sources list as VOA; verify against current official PDF

outbound_official.notes: Official source: Peru Ministerio de Relaciones Exteriores (RREE) publication 'Países que no exigen visa a peruanos', updated June 3, 2025. PDF hosted at cdn.www.gob.pe returned HTTP 403 during fetch; country list compiled from Andina (Peru's official news agency) and El Comercio articles that directly cite the same RREE source page. The official page itself (gob.pe) returned HTTP 418 bot-protection. The RREE page does not distinguish e-visa from visa-on-arrival for all countries - detailed categorization may require direct PDF access. Andean Community and Mercosur members may enter with DNI (national identity card) without a passport.

name: Peru

visa_free.notes: Tourist and business. Andean Community and Mercosur member - may enter with national ID card.
Tourist and business. Andean Community member - may enter with national ID card. Also entitled to 2-year Andean Community residence visa.
Tourist and business. Mercosur member - may enter with national ID card.
Tourist and business. Mercosur associated state - may enter with national ID card.
Tourist and business. Andean Community member - may enter with national ID card. Also entitled to 2-year Andean Community residence visa.
Tourist and business. Andean Community member - may enter with national ID card. Also entitled to 2-year Andean Community residence visa.
Tourist. Mercosur member - may enter with national ID card.
Tourist. Mercosur member - may enter with national ID card.
Tourist. US State Dept confirms no visa required for tourist visits.
Tourist. Confirmed by US State Department official travel page for Peru.
Tourist and business. Note: visa was temporarily suspended by DS N° 011-2024-RE then reinstated by DS N° 012-2024-RE.
Tourist. Only African country with visa-free access to Peru for ordinary passport holders.

Tourist and business. Tourist exemption has been in place under prior bilateral agreement. Business exemption (Calidad Migratoria de Negocios) for 183 days in a 12-month period added by Decreto Supremo N.° 020-2025-RE (published June 23, 2025, effective July 1, 2025). Ordinary passport required with minimum 6 months validity.

Tourist. NEW - added by Decreto Supremo N.° 023-2025-RE, effective July 4, 2025. Ordinary passport with minimum 6 months validity required. Up to 183 days in a 12-month period (single or multiple entries). Qatar's Ministry of Foreign Affairs confirmed on July 14, 2025.

Visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period. Based on EU-Peru Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement ratified by Peru (effective March 15, 2016). Covers ordinary, diplomatic, service, official and special passports. EUR-Lex CELEX:22016A0324(01).
Tourist and business. Visa-free within 180-day period.
Tourist. UK citizens do not need a Peruvian tourist visa.
Tourist. Holders of Hong Kong SAR ordinary passports.
Tourist. Holders of Taiwan ordinary passports.

visa_policy.notes: Peru's official visa exemption list is published by the Ministerio de Relaciones Exteriores (RREE) as 'Requerimientos y Exoneración de Visas para Ciudadanos Extranjeros con Pasaportes Ordinarios' (latest update Nov 24, 2025). The document is hosted at cdn.www.gob.pe. The gob.pe portal pages returned HTTP 418 and the PDF returned HTTP 403 during research, so El Peruano decree URLs and Qatar MFA confirmation are used for the 2025 additions. Peru does not currently offer an e-visa or visa-on-arrival for ordinary passport holders - those who need a visa must apply at a Peruvian consulate. A transit visa (visa de transeúnte) is required since Jan 2025 for those transiting Peru who are not already exempt from the tourist/business visa (DS 004-2025-RE). EU citizens are covered by the EU-Peru Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement (in force March 15, 2016), allowing 90 days in any 180-day period. Qatar was added by DS 023-2025-RE (effective July 4, 2025, 183 days tourism). Japan's business exemption was extended to 183 days by DS 020-2025-RE (effective July 1, 2025).

conditional_access.conditions: Chinese nationals holding a valid US visa with minimum 6 months remaining validity from date of arrival in Peru are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 069-2016-RE (published 01/09/2016).

Chinese nationals holding a valid Canadian visa with minimum 6 months remaining validity from date of arrival in Peru are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 069-2016-RE (published 01/09/2016).

Chinese nationals holding a valid UK visa with minimum 6 months remaining validity from date of arrival in Peru are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 069-2016-RE (published 01/09/2016).

Chinese nationals holding a valid Australian visa with minimum 6 months remaining validity from date of arrival in Peru are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 069-2016-RE (published 01/09/2016).

Chinese nationals holding a valid Schengen Area visa with minimum 6 months remaining validity from date of arrival in Peru are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 069-2016-RE (published 01/09/2016).

Chinese nationals holding valid US permanent residence (Green Card) are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 069-2016-RE (published 01/09/2016).

Chinese nationals holding valid Canadian permanent residence are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 069-2016-RE (published 01/09/2016).

Chinese nationals holding valid UK permanent residence (ILR/settlement) are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 069-2016-RE (published 01/09/2016).

Chinese nationals holding valid Australian permanent residence are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 069-2016-RE (published 01/09/2016).

credential.subtype: permanent residence in a Schengen Area member state

conditional_access.conditions: Chinese nationals holding valid permanent residence in a Schengen Area member state are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 069-2016-RE (published 01/09/2016).

Indian nationals holding a valid US visa with minimum 6 months remaining validity from date of arrival in Peru are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 006-2017-RE (published 06/03/2017).

Indian nationals holding a valid Canadian visa with minimum 6 months remaining validity from date of arrival in Peru are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 006-2017-RE (published 06/03/2017).

Indian nationals holding a valid UK visa with minimum 6 months remaining validity from date of arrival in Peru are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 006-2017-RE (published 06/03/2017).

Indian nationals holding a valid Australian visa with minimum 6 months remaining validity from date of arrival in Peru are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 006-2017-RE (published 06/03/2017).

Indian nationals holding a valid Schengen Area visa with minimum 6 months remaining validity from date of arrival in Peru are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 006-2017-RE (published 06/03/2017).

Indian nationals holding valid US permanent residence (Green Card) are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 006-2017-RE (published 06/03/2017).

Indian nationals holding valid Canadian permanent residence are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 006-2017-RE (published 06/03/2017).

Indian nationals holding valid UK permanent residence (ILR/settlement) are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 006-2017-RE (published 06/03/2017).

Indian nationals holding valid Australian permanent residence are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 006-2017-RE (published 06/03/2017).

credential.subtype: permanent residence in a Schengen Area member state

conditional_access.conditions: Indian nationals holding valid permanent residence in a Schengen Area member state are exempt from tourist/business visa requirement. Single or multiple entries within a 6-month period. Based on Decreto Supremo N° 006-2017-RE (published 06/03/2017).

Third-country nationals who normally require a Peruvian tourist visa are exempt if they hold valid permanent residence in Chile (a Pacific Alliance member state). Based on Decreto Supremo N° 061-2016-RE (published 24/07/2016), grounded in the Pacific Alliance Framework Agreement (signed 06/06/2012, in force 20/07/2015).

Third-country nationals who normally require a Peruvian tourist visa are exempt if they hold valid permanent residence in Colombia (a Pacific Alliance member state). Based on Decreto Supremo N° 061-2016-RE (published 24/07/2016), grounded in the Pacific Alliance Framework Agreement (signed 06/06/2012, in force 20/07/2015).

Third-country nationals who normally require a Peruvian tourist visa are exempt if they hold valid permanent residence in Mexico (a Pacific Alliance member state). Based on Decreto Supremo N° 061-2016-RE (published 24/07/2016), grounded in the Pacific Alliance Framework Agreement (signed 06/06/2012, in force 20/07/2015).

Holders of diplomatic, special, official, or service passports from countries that have concluded a visa suppression agreement (convenio de supresión de visas) with Peru are exempt from visa requirements. As of the official May 2015 RREE list, agreement partners include: Algeria, Argentina, Austria, Azerbaijan, Barbados, Belize, Belgium/Netherlands/Luxembourg (Benelux), Belarus, Bolivia, Brazil, Brunei, Bulgaria, Cambodia, Chile, China (diplomatic/special/service only - ordinary passport still requires visa), Colombia, South Korea, Costa Rica, Denmark, Ecuador, Egypt, El Salvador, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Russia, Philippines, Finland, France, Georgia, Greece, Guatemala, Guyana, Honduras, Hungary, India, Indonesia, Israel, Italy, Jamaica, Jordan, Kuwait, Laos, Latvia, Macedonia, Malaysia, Malta, Morocco, Mexico, Moldova, Montenegro, Mongolia, Nicaragua, Palestine, Panama, Paraguay, Poland, Portugal, Czech Republic, Dominican Republic, Romania, Serbia, Singapore, Thailand, Tunisia, Turkey, Ukraine, Uruguay, Venezuela, Vietnam, and others with more recent additions. This list may not be exhaustive - the official updated January 2024 PDF (cdn.www.gob.pe) returned HTTP 403 during research.

UK nationals holding diplomatic, official, or service passports are admitted with calidad migratoria Diplomática Temporal u Oficial Temporal for up to 183 calendar days (continuous or consecutive). This is a unilateral Peruvian decree issued in reciprocity for the UK's 2022 unilateral exemption of Peruvian citizens. Decreto Supremo N° 003-2024-RE, published 23/01/2024.

Bilateral agreement between Peru and Indonesia on visa exemption for holders of diplomatic, special, or service passports. Agreement signed in Lima on 14 November 2024; ratified by Peru via Decreto Supremo N° 006-2025-RE (published 05/02/2025). Exact stay duration to be confirmed from the published agreement text in Diario Oficial El Peruano per Article 2 of the decree.

Unilateral Peruvian exemption for holders of valid diplomatic and service passports of the Holy See (Vatican), based on reciprocity - the Vatican grants Peruvian diplomatic and official passport holders visa-free entry for short stays. Grounded in the bilateral framework established by the 1980 Peru–Holy See agreement. Published February 27, 2026 in Diario Oficial El Peruano, effective February 28, 2026 (DS 007-2026-RE). Migration status granted: Calidad Migratoria Diplomática Temporal u Oficial Temporal. Maximum stay: 90 days. Ordinary Holy See passports are NOT covered by this exemption.
conditional_access.notes: Primary source is DS 007-2026-RE (busquedas.elperuano.pe — page was not directly accessible during research but confirmed via Actualidad Penal legal aggregator mirror at actualidadpenal.pe). The 90-day max stay and 'Diplomática Temporal u Oficial Temporal' migration status were confirmed from the legal aggregator text. Andina (Peru's official news agency) also confirmed the decree. Source_official remains false until the El Peruano page is directly accessible.

cbi.notes: Peru does not currently offer a citizenship-by-investment program. A prior program was briefly established in 1992 but was closed within a year due to public opposition. No current official CBI program exists. Naturalization is available after 2 consecutive years of legal residence (this requirement is expected to increase to 5 years under Ley 32421 published August 2025 once implementing regulations take effect). Source: multiple official government references and DS confirming no active CBI program.

programs.name: Residencia para Inversionista (Investor Resident Visa)
programs.notes: Minimum investment of PEN 500,000 in a new or existing Peruvian company, made by international transfer through a Peruvian SBS-supervised financial institution. Applicant must commit to create at least 5 jobs for Peruvians within 1 year. Residency is granted for 365 days, renewable annually. After 2 years of residency, permanent residency may be applied for. Application fee: S/ 57.60 (code 07567 at Banco de la Nación). Processing time: 30 business days. Administered by Superintendencia Nacional de Migraciones.

programs.name: Calidad Migratoria de Rentista (Independent Means / Passive Income Resident Visa)
programs.notes: Requires proof of permanent monthly income of at least USD 1,000 from qualifying sources (pension, lifetime annuity, royalties, or dividends guaranteed for life). Income must be received through a Peruvian financial institution. An additional USD 500/month per dependent is required. Qualifying income does NOT include remote/freelance work, rental income, capital gains or interest. This visa grants permanent residency (indefinite validity). Governed by Decreto Supremo 002-2021-IN.

programs.name: Andean Community (CAN) Residence Visa
programs.notes: Available to nationals of Bolivia, Colombia, and Ecuador under Andean Community Decision 878 (Andean Migration Statute, in force August 2021). Two-year temporary residence visa. After 2 years, permanent residence may be applied for. Applicant must have clean criminal record and demonstrate means of support. Implemented in Peru by decree effective October 23, 2023.

programs.name: MERCOSUR Residence Visa
programs.notes: Available to nationals of MERCOSUR member and associated states (Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Ecuador, Paraguay, Uruguay). Peru ratified the MERCOSUR Residence Agreement effective January 1, 2024. Application can be made at a Peruvian consulate abroad or in-country. Two-year temporary residence, path to permanent residence after 2 years.

programs.name: Nómada Digital (Digital Nomad) Migration Category
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals who work remotely for companies domiciled outside Peru. Introduced by Decreto Legislativo 1582 (November 2023).
programs.processing_time: Not yet implemented as of June 2026
programs.notes: Announced in November 2023 and incorporated into Peru's Migration Law via Decreto Legislativo 1582. Would allow up to 365 days stay with renewal. However, the updated TUPA published September 2025 made no reference to this category, meaning the Digital Nomad Visa has not been implemented and applications are not yet accepted. Regulations are still pending.

agreements: Andean Community (Comunidad Andina - CAN) free movement and residence (Decision 878)

validation_notes: ADDED Qatar (QAT) to visa_free: DS 023-2025-RE (effective July 4, 2025) grants 183-day tourist visa exemption for ordinary passport holders with minimum 6 months validity. This entry was missing from the prior record. Confirmed from El Peruano (busquedas.elperuano.pe/dispositivo/NL/2415561-2) and Qatar MFA announcement.

CORRECTED Japan (JPN) max_stay_days from 90 to 183 and updated notes: DS 020-2025-RE (published June 23, 2025, effective July 1, 2025) added a 183-day business exemption (Calidad Migratoria de Negocios). The prior record only reflected the 90-day tourist exemption. The consolidated max_stay is now 183 days (tourist+business combined). Source: busquedas.elperuano.pe/dispositivo/NL/2412462-2.
ADDED El Peruano URLs for Qatar (NL/2415561-2) and Japan business exemption (NL/2412462-2) to source_urls.
ADDED Qatar MFA domain (mofa.gov.qa) to official_domains.
REMOVED gap note about the prior record not confirming no e-visa/VOA - this remains confirmed: Peru still offers no e-visa or visa-on-arrival for ordinary passport holders as of June 2026.
All other visa_free entries, conditional_access entries, cbi, rbi, fast_track, and agreements sections are unchanged from prior record as no new official information contradicting them was found.

[2026-06-27] RE-VERIFIED all major existing source URLs against live pages: busquedas.elperuano.pe DS 023-2025-RE (Qatar tourist exemption) and DS 020-2025-RE (Japan business exemption) both confirmed accessible and matching record claims. US State Dept (travel.state.gov/Peru) re-confirmed: US citizens visa-free for tourism <90 days, no e-visa or VOA mentioned.

[2026-06-27] ADDED Holy See (Vatican) diplomatic and service passport exemption to conditional_access: DS 007-2026-RE (published February 27, 2026) grants visa exemption to holders of valid Holy See diplomatic and service passports on reciprocity basis. Source confirmed via Andina (Peru's official news agency). Ordinary Holy See passports NOT covered. This entry was flagged as unconfirmed gap in prior run - now added with Andina as semi-official source pending direct El Peruano access.

[2026-06-27] CONFIRMED DS 026-2025-RE is a consular regulation amendment (not a new visa exemption country). CONFIRMED DS 016-2026-RE covers protection of Peruvian diplomats abroad (not a visa exemption). CONFIRMED DS 011-2026-RE is a Peru-Bolivia border module agreement (not a visa exemption). No new ordinary-passport visa-free countries found in 2025–2026 beyond Qatar and Japan (already in record).

[2026-06-27] CONFIRMED that China's visa-free policy announced in May 2025 applies to Peruvian nationals traveling to China (outbound) - it does NOT grant Chinese ordinary passport holders visa-free entry to Peru. Peru's existing conditional access for Chinese ordinary passport holders (via US/Schengen/Canada/UK/Australia visa or PR) remains unchanged.

[2026-06-29] RE-VERIFIED Qatar (DS 023-2025-RE) via busquedas.elperuano.pe — confirmed decree exempts Qatar nationals in 'calidad migratoria de turista'. Japan (DS 020-2025-RE) confirmed: exempts Japanese nationals in 'Calidad Migratoria de Negocios'. Both decrees accessible and matching existing record.

[2026-06-29] CORRECTED Holy See conditional_access entry: max_stay_days updated from null to 90. Migration status confirmed as 'Calidad Migratoria Diplomática Temporal u Oficial Temporal'. Effective date confirmed as February 28, 2026 (the day after publication of DS 007-2026-RE). Source confirmed via Actualidad Penal legal aggregator mirror of the decree text; busquedas.elperuano.pe/dispositivo/NL/2490894-1 appears to be the sumario page (not the decree itself). Source_official remains false until El Peruano is directly accessible.

[2026-06-29] SEARCHED for new ordinary-passport visa exemption decrees in 2026: no new countries found beyond Qatar and Japan (already in record). DS 007-2026-RE (Holy See) covers only diplomatic/service passports. No other RE decrees granting ordinary-passport exemptions in 2026 were found. gob.pe portal still returns HTTP 418; cdn.www.gob.pe PDF still returns HTTP 403.
gaps: The primary official source document (cdn.www.gob.pe PDF dated Nov 24 2025) returned HTTP 403 Forbidden and could not be directly scraped; the Nov 2025 PDF may contain additional updates not yet captured in this record.

All gob.pe tramite pages for investor and rentista visas returned HTTP 418 and could not be directly read; investment amounts and requirements are derived from aggregator sources that reference the official TUPA.
The exact minimum investment amount for the Investor Resident Visa (PEN 500,000) could not be verified verbatim from a directly accessible official page during this session.
No e-visa or visa-on-arrival facility was found for Peru; confirm there is none by checking the live gob.pe page.
Exact stay durations for some Caribbean and Oceania countries could not be independently verified from an accessible official page.
Digital Nomad Visa regulations are still pending as of the research date; implementation status should be rechecked.
Cuba, El Salvador, Haiti, Nicaragua, and Venezuela require visas - confirmed from multiple sources but not verified from the live official document.

Path to citizenship years for investor visa assumed at 4 (2 years temp + 2 years PR + naturalization at 2 years); the new Ley 32421 may change naturalization requirements to 5 years once regulations are issued.

Macau SAR conditional entry: Wikipedia's visa policy of Peru article states Macau SAR holders may enter visa-free up to 180 days if holding valid US/Canada/UK/Australia/Schengen visa or PR (same conditions as China/India). However, no dedicated official Peruvian decree was found during this research. The Nov 2025 official PDF lists Macau separately in the visa-free (unconditional) section; the conditional rule for Macau could not be confirmed from an accessible official source and was therefore omitted.

The official updated January 2024 visa suppression agreements list (cdn.www.gob.pe/uploads/document/file/5499838/...) returned HTTP 403 - exact stay durations and full country list for diplomatic/service passport exemptions verified only from the May 2015 RREE PDF. The UK and Indonesia entries were verified from more recent official decrees (DS 003-2024-RE and DS 006-2025-RE respectively). Other additions between 2015 and 2024 may not be captured.

The exact stay duration granted under the Indonesia bilateral visa suppression agreement (DS 006-2025-RE) was not specified in the ratification decree text; the agreement text itself was not published during the research session.

Holy See (DS 007-2026-RE): max_stay_days now set to 90 based on legal aggregator mirror. The primary busquedas.elperuano.pe URL for this decree was not directly loaded; the exact El Peruano page URL for DS 007-2026-RE is unconfirmed (sumario NL/2490894-1 resolves to a different PCM decree). Should be re-verified when the El Peruano search page is accessible.

Japan's prior 90-day tourist exemption under the historical bilateral agreement may remain in effect alongside the new 183-day business exemption; the Nov 2025 PDF may clarify whether tourist stay was also updated to 183 days. Record updated to 183 days based on DS 020-2025-RE which grants up to 183 days.

visa_types.name: Visa de Turista / Calidad Migratoria de Turista
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay visits for tourism, recreation, sport, health, study trips of up to 30 days, family visits, or transit. No lucrative activities permitted.
visa_types.notes: Nationals of visa-exempt countries enter without a visa for up to 90 or 183 days (depending on nationality) under the tourist/negocios category. Nationals requiring a visa must apply at a Peruvian consulate abroad. Stay granted at port of entry by immigration officer; typically up to 90 days, extendable to a maximum of 183 days total within a 365-day period. Peru does not offer a tourist e-visa or visa-on-arrival for ordinary passport holders requiring a visa - consulate application only. Governed by Decreto Legislativo N° 1350 and DS 007-2017-IN.

visa_types.name: Calidad Migratoria de Negocios
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay visits for business activities such as meetings, negotiations, contract signings, conferences, and market research. No employment or income-generating work permitted.
visa_types.notes: Visa-exempt nationals may enter for business purposes under the Negocios category (same exemption as tourist). Max stay up to 183 days in a 12-month period. Nationals requiring a visa must apply at a Peruvian consulate. Japan's business exemption (DS 020-2025-RE, effective July 1, 2025) explicitly names the Calidad Migratoria de Negocios category for up to 183 days.

visa_types.name: Visa de Tránsito / Calidad Migratoria de Transeúnte
visa_types.purpose: Permits transit through Peru's territory or international airport areas for passengers continuing to a third country.
visa_types.notes: Since January 22, 2025 (DS 004-2025-RE), a transit visa is required for third-country nationals transiting Peru who are not already exempt from the tourist/business visa. Nationals who are visa-exempt for tourist/business entry are also exempt from the transit visa requirement. Must be applied for at a Peruvian consulate abroad. Transit without visa (TWOV) is not available for those who require a visa.

visa_types.name: Calidad Migratoria de Trabajador
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes foreign nationals to reside in Peru and work for a Peruvian employer or under a Peruvian labor contract.
visa_types.notes: Work authorization must be obtained before the visa can be issued; the employer typically sponsors the application and must obtain prior labor authorization from Peru's Ministry of Labor (MTPE) in most cases. Renewable annually. Path to permanent residency after 2 consecutive years. Governed by DL 1350 and DS 007-2017-IN. Application at Peruvian consulate abroad or via change of status in-country at Migraciones.

visa_types.name: Calidad Migratoria de Estudiante
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign nationals to reside in Peru to pursue full-time studies at an officially recognized educational institution.
visa_types.notes: Applicant must have an acceptance letter from a Peruvian educational institution recognized by the Ministry of Education (MINEDU) or equivalent. Cannot be used for tourist-style language courses under 30 days (those fall under tourist category). Renewable annually for the duration of studies. Path to permanent residency after 2 consecutive years of continuous residence. Governed by DL 1350 and DS 007-2017-IN.

visa_types.name: Calidad Migratoria de Residente para Inversionista
visa_types.purpose: Grants temporary residency to foreign nationals who make a qualifying investment in a Peruvian company and commit to creating local employment.
visa_types.notes: Minimum investment of PEN 500,000 (approximately USD 130,000) in a new or existing Peruvian company via international bank transfer through a Peruvian SBS-supervised institution. Applicant must commit to create at least 5 jobs for Peruvian nationals within 1 year of obtaining residency. Application fee: S/ 57.60 (code 07567 at Banco de la Nación). Renewable annually. After 2 years, permanent residency may be applied for. Governed by DL 1350, DS 007-2017-IN, and DS 002-2021-IN.

visa_types.name: Calidad Migratoria de Rentista
visa_types.purpose: Grants permanent residency to foreign nationals who demonstrate a stable, permanent passive income from qualifying sources such as pensions, annuities, royalties, or dividends.
visa_types.notes: Requires proof of permanent monthly passive income of at least USD 1,000 from qualifying sources (pension, lifetime annuity, royalties guaranteed for life, or dividends). An additional USD 500/month per dependent is required. Income must be deposited through a Peruvian financial institution. Qualifying income does NOT include remote/freelance work, rental income, capital gains, or interest. Grants permanent residency (indefinite validity). Governed by DS 002-2021-IN.

visa_types.name: Calidad Migratoria de Nómada Digital
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes foreign nationals who work remotely for companies or clients domiciled outside Peru to reside and work in Peru without engaging in local employment.
visa_types.notes: Created by Decreto Legislativo N° 1582 (November 2023), which amended DL 1350 to add the Nómada Digital migration category. Would allow up to 365 days stay with renewal. However, as of June 2026, implementing regulations and TUPA procedures have not been published and this category is NOT yet operational - applications cannot be accepted. Status: legislated but unimplemented.

visa_types.name: Calidad Migratoria de Familiar de Residente / Reagrupación Familiar
visa_types.purpose: Permits family members of legally resident foreign nationals or Peruvian citizens to join them and reside in Peru.
visa_types.notes: Eligible family members include spouses/partners, minor children, and economically dependent parents of the principal resident or Peruvian national. Documents required include proof of relationship (marriage certificate, birth certificate) and proof of the principal's legal status. Renewable annually. Governed by DL 1350 and DS 007-2017-IN. Also available under Andean Community (CAN) Decision 878 for nationals of Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador.

visa_types.name: Calidad Migratoria de Religioso
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes foreign nationals who are members of officially recognized religious organizations to reside in Peru to carry out religious activities.
visa_types.notes: Must be a member of a religious organization formally registered in Peru. Requires a letter of support from the religious organization. Renewable annually. Governed by DL 1350 and DS 007-2017-IN.

visa_types.name: Calidad Migratoria Diplomática / Oficial Temporal
visa_types.purpose: Admits foreign diplomats, consular officers, and official government representatives and their families to Peru for diplomatic or official duties.
visa_types.notes: Diplomatic and official passport holders from countries with visa suppression agreements with Peru (covering diplomatic/official/service passports) enter visa-free. These include a large list of countries per the official RREE May 2015 table and subsequent updates. The UK specifically was granted a unilateral exemption for diplomatic/official/service passport holders for up to 183 days by DS 003-2024-RE (published January 23, 2024). Indonesia was added by bilateral agreement ratified by DS 006-2025-RE. Accredited diplomatic staff receive the 'Calidad Migratoria Diplomática' status through the Ministry of Foreign Relations, not through standard Migraciones channels.

visa_types.name: Calidad Migratoria Humanitaria
visa_types.purpose: Provides temporary protection and legal residency to foreign nationals who require humanitarian protection, including asylum seekers, refugees, stateless persons, and victims of trafficking.
visa_types.notes: Established under DL 1350. Covers refugees (under 1951 Refugee Convention), asylum seekers with pending applications, stateless persons, and victims of human trafficking. Administered jointly by Migraciones and the Comisión Especial para los Refugiados (CEPAR). Renewable annually while humanitarian need persists. Governed by DL 1350 and DS 007-2017-IN.

visa_types.name: Residencia de Comunidad Andina (CAN) / Estatuto Migratorio Andino
visa_types.purpose: Grants two-year temporary residency with path to permanent residency for nationals of Andean Community member states (Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador) based on reciprocal regional free movement agreement.
visa_types.notes: Available to nationals of Bolivia, Colombia, and Ecuador under Andean Community Decision 878 (Andean Migration Statute, in force August 2021). Implemented in Peru by decree effective October 23, 2023. Two-year temporary residence. After 2 years, permanent residence may be applied for. Applicant must have clean criminal record and demonstrate means of support. Can enter with national ID card (cedula/DNI) - no passport required.

visa_types.name: Residencia MERCOSUR / Acuerdo de Residencia MERCOSUR
visa_types.purpose: Grants two-year temporary residency with path to permanent residency for nationals of MERCOSUR member and associated states based on bilateral regional agreement.
visa_types.notes: Available to nationals of MERCOSUR member and associated states (Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Ecuador, Paraguay, Uruguay). Peru ratified the MERCOSUR Residence Agreement effective January 1, 2024. Can be applied for at a Peruvian consulate abroad or in-country at Migraciones. Two-year temporary residence, path to permanent residence after 2 years. MERCOSUR members may also enter with national ID card - no passport required.

sources_checked.note: HTTP 418 - could not be scraped
HTTP 403 - official document on Peru's MFA portal, dated Nov 24 2025; could not be directly scraped but URL confirmed as authoritative source
HTTP 403 - official list of countries with visa suppression agreements, dated Jan 26 2024; could not be directly scraped
DS N° 069-2016-RE - visa exemption for Chinese nationals for tourist/business, conditional on holding valid US/Canada/UK/Australia/Schengen visa (min 6 months) or PR. Max stay 180 days.
DS N° 006-2017-RE - visa exemption for Indian nationals for tourist/business, same conditions as China (DS 069-2016-RE). Max stay 180 days.
DS N° 061-2016-RE - tourist visa exemption for third-country nationals with permanent residence in Pacific Alliance states (Chile, Colombia, Mexico). Published 24/07/2016.
DS N° 003-2024-RE - unilateral visa exemption for UK diplomatic/official/service passport holders; 183 calendar days. Full text scraped and verified.

DS N° 006-2025-RE - ratification of bilateral agreement with Indonesia on visa exemption for holders of diplomatic/special/service passports; agreement signed 14/11/2024 in Lima. Full text scraped and verified.
Official May 2015 RREE table of visa suppression agreements for diplomatic/special/official/service passports. Full text scraped via Firecrawl.
DS N° 011-2024-RE - suspension of Mexico visa exemption
DS N° 012-2024-RE - reinstatement of Mexico visa exemption
DS N° 004-2025-RE - transit visa exemption for those already exempt from entry visa
DS N.° 020-2025-RE - Japan business (Negocios) visa exemption; 183 days in 12-month period; effective July 1, 2025. Published June 23, 2025.
DS N.° 023-2025-RE - Qatar tourist visa exemption; 183 days in 12-month period; ordinary passport with 6 months minimum validity; effective July 4, 2025. Published July 3, 2025.
EU-Peru Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement (in force March 15, 2016)
US State Dept travel info for Peru - confirms no visa required for US citizens, max stay 90 days (up to 183 days by officials)
Qatar Ministry of Foreign Affairs confirms Peru visa exemption for Qatari ordinary passport holders; 183 days; passport valid min 6 months.
DS N° 020-2025-RE - confirmed names 'Calidad Migratoria de Negocios' as the business migration category; Japan exempted for 183 days in this category.
DS N° 023-2025-RE - confirmed 'tourist' (turista) migration category for Qatar exemption; 183 days.
DS N° 003-2024-RE - confirmed 'Calidad Migratoria Diplomática Temporal u Oficial Temporal' category for UK diplomatic/official/service passport holders; 183 days.

Andina (Peru's official news agency) report on DS 007-2026-RE - Peru exempts Holy See diplomatic and service passport holders from visa requirement, published February 27, 2026, on reciprocity basis. Semi-official source used since busquedas.elperuano.pe page for DS 007-2026-RE was not directly accessible during this session. [2026-06-27]

[2026-06-29] Legal aggregator mirror of DS 007-2026-RE text. Confirmed: Holy See diplomatic and service passport holders exempted from visa requirement; migration status 'Diplomática Temporal u Oficial Temporal'; maximum stay 90 days; effective February 28, 2026 (day after publication). Ordinary passports NOT covered. Source is a third-party legal portal, not the primary official gazette.
